decision: Keep this change at the public interface layer and do not wire formalization execution into `BaseAgent` yet.
rationale: The user explicitly requested a minimal interfaces-plus-SDK change. The existing runtime has no formalization hook surface yet, so introducing runtime behavior here would increase blast radius and couple an unfinished API to production loop logic.
alternatives_considered: Modify `BaseAgent` immediately to accept formalization layers.
Add only empty protocols with no shared behavior.

decision: Model the formalization surface as abstract base classes plus small supporting dataclasses instead of pure protocols.
rationale: The requested design depends on shared default behavior such as `describe()` and default parameter-section rendering, which protocols do not express cleanly. A documentation-heavy ABC layer still fits the interfaces package because it defines construction and behavioral contracts rather than concrete runtime implementations.
alternatives_considered: Use `Protocol` only and force every implementation to duplicate default behavior.
Put the abstractions directly into the agents runtime package.

decision: Make `harnessiq.shared` export lazily instead of eagerly importing manifests and tools at package import time.
rationale: The new interface module exposed a pre-existing circular import path: importing `harnessiq.interfaces` could re-enter the package through `harnessiq.shared` and provider/tool modules before contract symbols were bound. Lazy exports fix the import graph without changing the public names.
alternatives_considered: Patch many provider and tool modules to import submodules instead of `harnessiq.interfaces`.
Avoid public package imports in tests and leave the SDK import path fragile.
